label: Certainty Levels
description: Canonical certainty scale used by SER and EO confidence fields.
usageNotes: Use `unknown` only when curation cannot reliably classify certainty.
This scale is orthogonal to evidence type and evidence level.

entries.label: Very High
entries.description: Extremely high confidence that the estimated effect or recommendation is stable.
usageNotes: Rare; reserve for exceptionally stable directive evidence or convergent mature synthesis.

entries.label: High
entries.description: High confidence that the estimated effect is close to the truth.

entries.label: Moderate
entries.description: Moderate confidence with some possibility that the estimate will change.
usageNotes: Default when evidence is useful but not fully stable.

entries.label: Low
entries.description: Limited confidence in the estimate or recommendation.
usageNotes: Use when several uncertainty sources remain unresolved.

entries.label: Very Low
entries.description: Very limited confidence in the estimate or recommendation.
usageNotes: Should strongly reduce execution readiness unless governed.

entries.label: Unknown
entries.description: Certainty has not yet been determined or cannot be assessed.
usageNotes: Temporary curation state, not a desired steady-state production value.
